'''Will Robinson''' (born 20 February 1971) is an Australian [[rugby league]] footballer who played professionally in England and Australia.

==Playing career==
Robinson started his career with the [[Balmain Tigers]] in 1990, debuting against [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]]. In 1992 Robinson played for the [[Indigenous Australian rugby league team]] in the [[1992 Pacific Cup (rugby league)|1992 Pacific Cup]].<ref name=book>{{cite book
| last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood
| title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
| publisher =Huia Publishers
| year = 2008
| pages = 276–279
| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
| isbn =9781869693312}}</ref> He was named in the team of the tournament, on the bench. Usually a five eighth or halfback in 1994, his final season with the Tigers, he moved into the centres.

He then spent two seasons with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], also playing at {{rlp|hk}}. Robinson spent 1997 and 1998 with the [[Illawarra Steelers]]. Robinson played in Illawarra's final ever game in the top grade which was a 25–24 loss against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] in Round 24 1998 at [[WIN Stadium]].

Robinson then moved to England, spending 1999 with the [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] before joining [[Hull FC]] for the 2000 season.

Robinson then returned to his hometown of Coonabarabran, where he led the Unicorns to its inaugural Group 4 First Grade Premiership victory over the North Tamworth Bears in 2003.
